domain: Model Routing, Cascading & Orchestration for SLMs

gaps.title: Edge-Native SLM Cascading Router

demand_signals.signal: Data-augmented model routing framework for efficient LLM deployment in edge-cloud environments published in Journal of Supercomputing
Comprehensive survey on 'Collaborative Inference and Learning between Edge SLMs and Cloud LLMs' — highlights cascading as critical open challenge
Dell predicts dramatic shift from LLMs to SLMs optimized for edge environments in 2026
Dedicated article on 'The Future of LLM Routing: On-device, Edge AI, and Federated Models' — confirms market anticipation
AWS blog on edge deployment of fine-tuned SLMs for telco operations — real enterprise use case
Gartner predicts by 2027, organizations will use small task-specific AI models 3x more than general-purpose LLMs
Hybrid edge-cloud for agentic AI workloads yields energy savings up to 75% and cost reductions exceeding 80%

gaps.demand_summary: Massive research activity (multiple 2025 papers, surveys), strong industry signals (Dell, AWS, Gartner), and clear cost/energy benefits. No production tool exists yet despite overwhelming evidence of need.

gaps.title: Confidence-Calibrated Cascading Framework

demand_signals.signal: Dedicated survey: 'Dynamic Model Routing and Cascading for Efficient LLM Inference' published March 2026
Conference paper on 'Large Language Model Cascades with Mixture of Thought Representations' at ICLR
Bi-directional Model Cascading with Proxy Confidence — novel approach using auxiliary models to approximate larger model confidence
Cost-Saving LLM Cascades with Early Abstention — up to 16x efficiency gains
Improving Model Cascades Through Confidence Tuning — peer-reviewed research on calibrated cascading

gaps.demand_summary: Extremely active research area with a dedicated 2026 survey, multiple ICLR/arXiv papers, and demonstrated 16x efficiency gains. Research-to-product gap is wide — strong productization opportunity.

gaps.title: Per-User Personalized Routing

demand_signals.signal: PersonalizedRouter: graph-based framework outperforms existing methods by 15-60% — published at TMLR
Dynamic LLM Routing and Selection based on User Preferences — balancing performance, cost, and ethics
AWS published multi-LLM routing strategies guide including per-tenant differentiation for SaaS
LLMRouter includes PersonalizedRouter as one of its 16+ router families — academic validation

gaps.demand_summary: Strong academic interest with dedicated papers and framework support. AWS validates enterprise demand. However, LLMRouter already includes personalized routing, reducing the gap somewhat.

gaps.title: Multi-Step Agentic Workflow Router

demand_signals.signal: DAAO: Difficulty-Aware Agentic Orchestration framework for dynamic LLM routing in multi-step workflows
Helium: workflow-aware serving layer that models agentic workflows as query plans with LLM as first-class operators
Dedicated tutorial on 'AI Agent Routing: Tutorial & Best Practices' — confirms market demand
Anthropic published 'Building Effective Agents' guide highlighting multi-step orchestration patterns
LLM Hub Show HN: multi-model AI orchestration that decomposes requests into sub-tasks and routes each to best model

gaps.demand_summary: Explosive demand driven by the agentic AI wave. Multiple research frameworks (DAAO, Helium), industry guides (Anthropic, Patronus), and HN projects confirm this as one of the hottest gaps. 2026 will be the year of agentic routing.

gaps.title: Domain-Specialized Routing (Code, Legal, Medical, etc.)

demand_signals.signal: Major survey on 'Domain Specialization as the Key to Make Large Language Models Disruptive'
45%+ of AmLaw 200 firms in 2025 exploring domain-tuned models for contract review and case prediction
Article: 'The Future is Domain Specific: Finance, Healthcare, Legal LLMs' — confirms vertical specialization trend
IBM published dedicated explainer on domain-specific LLMs, confirming enterprise adoption trend

gaps.demand_summary: Strong enterprise demand especially in legal (45% of top firms) and healthcare. Domain specialization is a major trend, but routing across domains (vs. fine-tuning per domain) is a less-explored angle with high potential.

gaps.title: Open-Source Quality-Aware Router with Evaluation Loop

demand_signals.signal: LLMRouter crossed 1K GitHub stars, 16+ router architectures, unified CLI — but lacks integrated eval feedback loop
RouteLLM reduces costs 85% while maintaining 95% GPT-4 quality — but no automatic feedback loop
NadirClaw open-source LLM router saves 40-70% — growing ecosystem confirms demand
Multiple Show HN posts on LLM routers in 2025 (PureRouter, Arch-Router, LLMRouter, Any-LLM) — high community interest
'Lessons from building an intelligent LLM router' — practical experience-sharing post confirms active builder community

gaps.demand_summary: Very active OSS ecosystem with multiple projects and HN traction. The gap has narrowed (LLMRouter is comprehensive) but the specific eval-feedback-loop integration remains unaddressed. The 'route -> evaluate -> update' closed loop is still missing.

gaps.title: Budget-Constrained Adaptive Routing Optimizer

demand_signals.signal: Dedicated paper: 'Adaptive LLM Routing under Budget Constraints' using contextual bandit approach
Paper featured on Hugging Face papers page — community interest validated
Portkey published guide on implementing budget limits and alerts in LLM apps — confirms user demand
Developer blog post 'How I Cut My LLM Costs 60% With a Local Router' — confirms practitioners actively seeking budget optimization
Multiple 2026 cost optimization guides cite 60-85% savings potential — massive market interest in cost control

gaps.demand_summary: Strong demand from cost-conscious teams. Academic research exists but no product implements adaptive budget optimization (only hard caps). The gap between static budget caps and dynamic optimization is real and validated.

gaps.title: Multi-Modal Routing (Text + Image + Audio + Video)

demand_signals.signal: Paper on 'Smart Routing for Multimodal Video Retrieval: When to Search What' — modality-aware routing research
LLMRouter v0.2 added multimodal routing support (Video/Image + Text) — confirms demand from OSS community
Guide on multimodal AI development for text, images, audio, video — market is ready for multimodal tools

gaps.demand_summary: Moderate demand. LLMRouter already added multimodal support, reducing the gap. Research exists but multimodal routing is still nascent — the market is more focused on multimodal models than multimodal routing specifically.

gaps.title: Compliance-Aware Routing (GDPR, HIPAA, Data Residency)

demand_signals.signal: Security compliance checklist for LLM gateways covering SOC 2, HIPAA, GDPR — confirms enterprise need
TrueFoundry's '6 Best LLM Gateways in 2026' highlights compliance as key differentiator
Comprehensive guide on AI data residency requirements by region — enterprise compliance demand is massive
AWS European Sovereign Cloud launched January 2026 — infrastructure is catching up to compliance demand

gaps.demand_summary: Strong enterprise demand, especially in healthcare/finance/EU. Current solutions offer compliance features but not compliance-aware intelligent routing (combining quality optimization with compliance constraints). Niche but high-value.

gaps.title: Real-Time Routing Benchmarks and Model Performance Tracker

demand_signals.signal: LiveBench provides live LLM benchmarks — but generic, not per-customer workload
LangSmith offers LLM evaluation platform for production — partial overlap but not routing-integrated
Article on 'How to Build AI Benchmarks That Evolve' — confirms need for adaptive evaluation
Comparison dashboard of AI models across intelligence, performance, and price — generic benchmarking exists

gaps.demand_summary: Generic benchmarking tools exist (LiveBench, Artificial Analysis) and eval platforms exist (LangSmith), but none integrate continuous task-specific benchmarking with automatic routing policy updates. The closed-loop gap persists.

gaps.title: Hybrid Local/Cloud Router with Privacy Preservation

demand_signals.signal: Tutorial: 'PII-aware routing: how to use cloud AI and keep your sensitive data local' — developer demand confirmed
PrivateLoRA: partitions LLM with early layers on-device, compressed activations to cloud — research on privacy-preserving architecture
LLM-Driven Intent-Based Privacy-Aware Orchestration Across the Cloud-Edge Continuum
llm-privacy-stack: curated list of privacy-preserving AI/LLM tools — community actively building in this space

gaps.demand_summary: Growing demand driven by enterprise privacy requirements. Research and developer community are actively exploring PII-aware routing and privacy-preserving architectures. Overlaps significantly with GAP-001 (edge routing) but focuses on the privacy angle.

gaps.title: Routing-as-SDK for Framework Integration

demand_signals.signal: 14+ AI agent frameworks exist (LangChain, CrewAI, AutoGen, LlamaIndex) — massive integration surface area
Google ADK (2025) integrates with LangChain, LlamaIndex, CrewAI through LiteLLM — partial solution emerging
NVIDIA NeMo announced as framework-agnostic platform working with CrewAI, Haystack, LangChain, LlamaIndex
'LLM Orchestration in 2026: Top 22 frameworks and gateways' — confirms fragmented landscape needing unification

gaps.demand_summary: Moderate demand. The gap is being partially addressed by Google ADK and NVIDIA NeMo providing framework integrations. LiteLLM already serves as a de facto routing SDK. The remaining gap is framework-aware intelligent routing (not just API abstraction).

gaps.title: Cost-Quality Pareto Frontier Visualization and Exploration

demand_signals.signal: Live visualization of LLM Pareto frontier showing optimal performance-to-cost ratio — open source project exists
Post 'LLM Pareto Frontier But Live' — community interest in interactive cost-quality visualization
'Beyond the Pareto Frontier: Pricing LLM Mistakes in the Real World' — evolving thinking beyond simple cost-quality
Comparison dashboard of AI models across intelligence, performance, and price — basic version exists

gaps.demand_summary: Moderate interest but basic solutions already exist (Pareto frontier visualizers, Artificial Analysis). The gap for workload-specific Pareto exploration is real but niche. This is more of a feature for existing platforms than a standalone product.

gaps.title: Streaming/Real-Time Routing with Mid-Response Escalation

demand_signals.signal: Multi-model AI chat app supporting model switching in real-time — but user-initiated, not automatic quality-based
Speculative Cascades research from Google hints at token-level model switching — internal research only
All major LLM providers support streaming responses via SSE — infrastructure is ready for mid-stream switching

gaps.demand_summary: Technically fascinating but limited explicit demand signals. Mid-response escalation is research-grade (Google Speculative Cascades). The technical complexity is high and practical benefits over pre-generation routing are unclear for most use cases.

gaps.title: Fine-Tuned SLM Router Trainer (AutoML for Routing)

demand_signals.signal: NVIDIA LLM Router Blueprint: offers intent-based and auto-routing strategies with neural network training
Includes complete data generation pipeline for training data from 11 benchmark datasets with automatic API calling
Published as ICLR 2025 conference paper — training routers from preference data, reducing costs 85%
Blog post on 'Building an LLM Router for High-Quality and Cost-Effective Responses' — practical training guide

gaps.demand_summary: Demand exists but the gap is shrinking. LLMRouter and NVIDIA provide training pipelines. RouteLLM (ICLR 2025) popularized router training. The remaining gap is a true one-click AutoML experience for non-ML teams, but the bar is lower now.
